coding_notes: Gemini's original response was truncated; re-run at higher max_tokens=5000 produced full coverage. All sub-claims now codable. Anthropic was also re-run with the higher cap and produced more complete content. Verdicts below reflect re-run responses.
ground_truth_notes: Gout flare-trigger priming is an active research question; consensus does not exist on which mechanism is dominant. Best-supported in current literature (per Joosten et al. 2010 Arthritis Rheum + Crișan et al. 2016 Ann Rheum Dis): TLR2/FFA priming + soluble urate co-priming; gut-LPS translocation is plausible but less directly demonstrated for gout-flare context. The 'top mechanism for in-vivo test' has no single right answer.

claims.claim_text: Mechanism #1 includes gut-derived LPS / endotoxin translocation acting via TLR4 on macrophages.
At least one named mechanism involves saturated free fatty acids (FFAs) priming via TLR2 (Joosten 2010 axis).

gemini.value_quoted: Mechanism 3: palmitate/stearate -> TLR2/TLR4, cites Shi 2006 (NOT Joosten)

claims.claim_text: DAMPs (HMGB1, S100A8/A9, or related) are named as a candidate priming mechanism.

openai.value_quoted: (did not list DAMPs as separate top-3; mentioned only in passing)

anthropic.value_quoted: (replaced by soluble urate priming as third mechanism)

claims.claim_text: Soluble urate itself is named as a priming-signal candidate.
Top mechanism prioritized for in-vivo test is gut-LPS / TLR4.

deepseek.value_quoted: Top: TLR4 antagonist (TAK-242) in hyperuricemic flare model

gemini.value_quoted: Top priority: Mechanism 3 (FFAs), human dietary challenge with monocyte IL-1β assay

anthropic.value_quoted: Anthropic explicitly prioritized Mechanism 2 (FFA/TLR2) OVER LPS/TLR4, citing Joosten 2010 cleaner causal evidence and eritoran-failure precedent

claims.claim_text: Alternative non-priming explanation: protein-corona / crystal-shedding / bioavailability change of MSU.

deepseek.value_quoted: Protein corona / granuloma disruption as signal-2 modulation

gemini.value_quoted: Modulation of MSU Crystal Bioavailability via ApoB protein corona
